content: We can reframe catastrophic forgetting mitigation in online meta-learning as an attention-gated episodic pruning problem, where Transformer-style sparse attention from Topic B dynamically gates which old meta-learning task parameters are consolidated versus reactivated, rather than relying on fixed regularization to protect weights. This creates a curiosity-aligned consolidation pipeline where attention prioritizes storing surprising, high-learning-gain new tasks that expand the agent's meta-knowledge instead of overwriting useful prior representations.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
Transformer attention mechanism for episodic memory
trigger_topic: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
